About This Dashboard
What It Does
Short-term momentum ranker scoring 700+ tickers on returns (1d to 1y), SMA gate (above 30/50/100/200 day), ratio consistency across timeframes, and resilience on bad SPY days. Higher composite scores indicate stronger, more consistent short-term momentum.
Today's Reading
# Market Analysis: momentum-ranker CLMT dominates today's ranking at #1 with a composite score of 96.5, driven by exceptional consistency across all metrics—it passes 4 of 5 ratio tests, trades above all major moving averages, and delivers a 1-year return of 158.85% while outperforming SPY by 146.96% over the same period. Its ratio structure is notably clean with strong intermediate-term acceleration (ratio_3m_1m of 12.75) and a healthy bad_spy_score of 0.7245, indicating it holds up relatively well during market downturns. In contrast, DOW ranks #2 with a score of 95.1 but shows structural weakness: despite strong short-term momentum (77.17% in 3 months), its 1-year return collapses to just 14.83%, and it carries a concerning bad_spy_score of 2.4685—nearly 3.4x higher than CLMT—suggesting vulnerability when equities sell off. The correlation vectors reveal CLMT leans toward commodity and materials exposure (DBA and IAU correlations near +0.17) while avoiding tech and consumer discretionary, whereas DOW shows tight coupling to cyclicals (XLB at +0.537), which amplifies downside risk during market
